exit_criteria: Accepted wait queue requirements are recorded.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: Implementation should not rely on chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset
Feedback build contains requirements and non-goals.
next_loop.handoff: Use this build to update claim docs and implement wait queue support.

requirements.text: CodeWiki claims must support a wait queue for sessions that cannot safely claim needed scopes because another active write claim blocks them.
Waiting sessions must have their own TTL/heartbeat so dead waiters expire and do not create stale wakeups.
When blocking claims release or expire, matching waiters should become ready and be visible through claim state/graph/status so adapters can resume from artifacts or start a fresh handoff.
Wake behavior must not revive stale chat context as truth; resumed work starts from task/build/scope artifacts and current graph state.

summary: Accepted claim wait queue direction: blocked agents can register waiters on claimed scopes, keep waiter leases alive, and get fresh wake handoffs when claims release or expire.

diff_table.current_state: Overlapping write claims block new claims and leave agents with no durable way to wait on the artifact/scope they need.
diff_table.desired_state: Add claim waiters that register blocked desired scopes and show pending/ready state.
diff_table.rationale: Agents need observable coordination instead of silent deadlock or manual polling.
diff_table.risk: Waiters could become stale if they lack expiry.

diff_table.current_state: Claim heartbeat only proves the holder is alive.
diff_table.desired_state: Reuse heartbeat/release for WAIT ids so waiting agents can keep or cancel their queue entries.
diff_table.rationale: Waiter leases need the same temporary-runtime lifecycle as claims.
diff_table.risk: Must avoid confusing claim holder heartbeat with waiter heartbeat in UI/state.

diff_table.current_state: Claim release/expiry only removes a blocker; it does not create a wake signal.
diff_table.desired_state: Release/expiry refreshes matching waiters and marks unblocked waiters ready.
diff_table.rationale: Ready waiters give adapters/status/UI a clear next action.
diff_table.risk: Wake must use fresh artifact context, not old chat memory.

accepted_decisions.summary: Implement a claim wait queue, not a force-release/override mechanism.
Waiters are temporary runtime coordination records with TTL and heartbeat.
Existing heartbeat/release actions should work for WAIT ids where practical.
Ready wake state must route agents through fresh task/build/scope context rather than stale waiting context.

assumptions: Initial implementation can expose ready waiters through claim tool output, graph views, and status state without spawning sessions automatically.
A later adapter task can turn ready waiters into automatic session_handoff if policy allows.
non_goals: No automatic takeover of another session's claim.
No reviving stale chat memory as canonical context.
